raw.objective: “to examine the influence of weight, height, surface area, autoinduction, age, gender, and comedication upon changes in clearance of the bioavailable fraction of carbamazepine”
raw.covariates: Tested: WT, HT, BSA, autoinduction, age, gender, and comedications. Included: BSA, dose on CL; WT on Vd
raw.observations: Variability of Ka and Vd extremely large, bootstrap results suggest that these values could be poorly estimated. As the results of the bootstrap indicate, the estimates of thetas and etas of Ka and Vd are very inaccurate and should not be trusted, the extreme values for their respective etas would result in overprediction of variability and thus reduce the usefulness of the model for future predictions
raw.equations: CL/F=((theta1*SA) + (theta4*DW))*exp(eta1) Vd/F=theta2*WT*exp(eta2) Ka=theta3*exp(eta3) Cobs=Cipred + eps1 DW=CBZ dose/kg SA=surface area calculated using: sqrt((HT*WT)/3600)
raw.parameter_values: theta1=2.24 theta2=0.37 theta3=0.013 theta4=0.047 omega1=0.053 omega2=1.36 omega3=6.11 sigma1=0.0331

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending
Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending
Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: CL/F=((theta1*SA) + (theta4*DW))*exp(eta1) Vd/F=theta2*WT*exp(eta2) Ka=theta3*exp(eta3) Cobs=Cipred + eps1 DW=CBZ dose/kg SA=surface area calculated using: sqrt((HT*WT)/3600)
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to examine the influence of weight, height, surface area, autoinduction, age, gender, and comedication upon changes in clearance of the bioavailable fraction of carbamazepine”

covariates.raw: Tested: WT, HT, BSA, autoinduction, age, gender, and comedications. Included: BSA, dose on CL; WT on Vd

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
